title: Mobile Responsiveness Fixes & Admin Login Button Label Update

requirements.text: Make the entire public-facing site fully responsive on mobile devices. Audit and fix all layout, spacing, typography, and component issues across: Header (navigation, anchor links, admin panel link), Home/Hero section (particle canvas, hero image, site title), About section (two-column layout should stack vertically on mobile), Services grid, Clinics grid, SocialMedia section, and Footer. Ensure touch targets are adequately sized, text does not overflow, images scale correctly, and grid/flex layouts collapse gracefully at small screen widths (sm breakpoint and below).

quotes: The UI is not rendering properly on mobile, make it fully responsive.

acceptanceCriteria: All public pages render correctly on 375px wide viewport (mobile)
No horizontal overflow or clipped content on mobile
Two-column About section layout stacks to single column on mobile
Services and Clinics grids collapse to 1 column on mobile
Header navigation is accessible and usable on mobile
Footer renders correctly without overflow on mobile
Touch targets (buttons, links) are at least 44px in height/width on mobile

requirements.text: In the AdminPanel page and Header component, change the admin login button label from 'Admin' to 'Login'. The button's behavior, styling, and all authentication/session logic must remain exactly the same — only the visible text label changes.

quotes: The admin login button should say "Login" not "Admin"

acceptanceCriteria: The admin panel access button displays 'Login' as its label
Clicking the button still navigates to the admin panel / triggers the login flow as before
No changes to authentication logic, session storage, or login form behavior

constraints: Do not modify any authentication or session management logic
Do not alter login preferences, credential handling, or admin access control
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Adding new features to the admin panel
Changing the admin login form fields or authentication flow
